from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
from app.db.status import VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_empty_and_no_match():
    # No messages -> None
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

    # Messages with no relevant keywords -> None
    messages = [{"message": "Hello, just checking in."}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None


def test_detect_stage_negating_pattern_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I can't make it tomorrow."},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION


def test_detect_stage_booking_with_time_triggers_booked():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Let's meet at 3pm tomorrow."},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_discussion_after_booking_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Confirmed for 2pm on 12/05."},
        {"message": "Actually, can we reschedule?"},
    ]
    # Because the last message contains discussion + negating or "instead" pattern,
    # it should return VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_or_responsive():
    # No landlord messages, previous is valid attitude
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="friendly") == "friendly"
    # No landlord messages, previous is invalid attitude
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="unknown") == "responsive"
    # No landlord messages, no previous
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([]) == "responsive"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_aggressive_pattern_detected():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Please stop wasting my time."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "This is serious only."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_time_gap_returns_slow_reply():
    now = datetime.now()
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": now - timedelta(days=2)},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Are you interested?", "created_at": now},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "slow_reply"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_pattern_priority_and_cold_pattern():
    # Aggressive pattern takes priority over suspicious, helpful, friendly, cold
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "You are rude."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Thanks for your interest."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Yes"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"

    # Suspicious pattern takes priority over helpful, friendly, cold
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Who are you?"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "I can show you the flat."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Ok"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "suspicious"

    # Helpful pattern takes priority over friendly, cold
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Happy to arrange a viewing."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Lovely place."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Ok"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "helpful"

    # Friendly pattern takes priority over cold
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Great!"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Ok"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "friendly"

    # Cold pattern matched only if no higher priority patterns
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Yes"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "cold"

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
from app.db.status import VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_empty_messages():
    # No messages should return None
    assert detect_stage([]) is None


def test_detect_stage_negating_pattern_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I need to cancel the appointment"},
        {"message": "Can we reschedule?"},
    ]
    # Latest message contains negating pattern "cancel"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION


def test_detect_stage_booking_with_time_triggers_booked():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Let's meet at 3pm tomorrow"},
        {"message": "Confirmed, see you then"},
    ]
    # Latest message contains booking pattern and time, should be booked
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_discussion_after_booking_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 2pm on 12/12"},
        {"message": "Actually, can we rearrange?"},
    ]
    # Discussion patterns combined with negating or rearranging triggers discussion stage
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count

def test_outbound_count_empty_list():
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_outbound_count_none_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0

def test_outbound_count_mixed_senders():
    messages = [
        {"from": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"from": "user", "message": "Hi"},
        {"direction": "outbound", "content": "Is the place available?"},
        {"from": "tenant", "message": "Yes, I am interested"},
        {"from": "inbound", "message": "Please call me"},
        {"from": "ai", "message": "I can help you"},
        {"from": "other", "message": "Ignore me"},
    ]
    # outbound_count should count user, tenant, outbound, ai only
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_outbound_count_case_insensitivity_and_missing_keys():
    messages = [
        {"from": "User", "message": "Hello"},  # case insensitive
        {"direction": "OUTBOUND", "message": "Message"},  # case insensitive
        {"from": "Tenant", "message": "Interested"},  # case insensitive
        {"from": "AI", "message": "Reply"},  # case insensitive
        {"from": "LANDLORD", "message": "Not counted"},
        {"content": "No sender key"},  # no sender keys, should not count
        {"from": None, "message": "None sender"},  # None sender, should not count
    ]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

from app.ai.personas import should_share_phone_now as phone_shared_state

def test_phone_shared_state_immediate_type_allows_early_sharing():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "immediate", "conversation_style": "direct_number_request"}
    # outbound_count 0 and 1 should allow sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage=None) is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage=None) is True
    # outbound_count 2 should disallow sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage=None) is False

def test_phone_shared_state_delayed_type_requires_viewing_booked_and_outbound_count():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"}
    # outbound_count less than 2 or stage not VIEWING_BOOKED disallows sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is False
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage="PRE_VIEWING") is False
    # outbound_count >= 2 and stage VIEWING_BOOKED allows sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True

def test_phone_shared_state_viewing_first_type_requires_viewing_booked_only():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "viewing_first"}
    # Only stage VIEWING_BOOKED allows sharing regardless of outbound_count
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=5, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=5, stage="PRE_VIEWING") is False

def test_phone_shared_state_adaptive_type_allows_sharing_on_viewing_or_high_drive_and_outbound():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "adaptive"}
    # stage VIEWING_BOOKED allows sharing regardless of outbound_count or drive_distance_high
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    # drive_distance_high True and outbound_count >=1 allows sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage="PRE_VIEWING", drive_distance_high=True) is True
    # drive_distance_high False and outbound_count <1 disallows sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="PRE_VIEWING", drive_distance_high=False) is False
    # phone_shared True always disallows sharing
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=True, outbound_count=10, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is False

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
from app.db.status import VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_empty_messages():
    # No messages -> None
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None


def test_detect_stage_negating_pattern_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"body": "I can't make it tomorrow"},
        {"content": "Are you available on Monday?"},
    ]
    # Latest message contains negating pattern -> VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION


def test_detect_stage_booking_with_time_triggers_booked():
    messages = [
        {"body": "Let's meet at 3pm tomorrow"},
        {"body": "Confirmed, see you then"},
    ]
    # Latest message contains booking pattern and time -> VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_detect_stage_discussion_after_booking_triggers_discussion():
    messages = [
        {"body": "Booked for 5pm on 12/12"},
        {"body": "Can we rearrange to 6pm instead?"},
    ]
    # Discussion pattern + negating or "instead" after booking -> VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

from app.ai.personas import get_conversation_style
import pytest


def test_get_conversation_style_valid_key_returns_correct_dict():
    style = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    assert style["label"] == "Friendly viewing first"
    assert style["phone_fetching_type"] == "viewing_first"
    assert "strategy" in style
    assert style["conversation_goal"].startswith("book a viewing")


def test_get_conversation_style_with_alias_key_returns_key_itself_and_raises_keyerror():
    # Since normalize_conversation_style returns the input if not in CONVERSATION_STYLES,
    # get_conversation_style will raise KeyError for alias keys.
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        get_conversation_style("viewing_first")


def test_get_conversation_style_with_empty_string_raises_keyerror():
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        get_conversation_style("")


def test_get_conversation_style_with_unknown_style_raises_keyerror():
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        get_conversation_style("non_existent_style")

from app.ai.personas import should_share_phone_now


def test_should_share_phone_now_returns_false_if_phone_already_shared():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123"}
    result = should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=True, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED")
    assert result is False


def test_should_share_phone_now_immediate_type_allows_first_outbound_only():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "immediate"}
    # outbound_count 0 and 1 should return True
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0) is True
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1) is True
    # outbound_count 2 should return False (off-by-one check)
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2) is False


def test_should_share_phone_now_delayed_type_requires_stage_and_outbound_count():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"}
    # outbound_count less than 2 returns False even if stage is VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is False
    # outbound_count 2 or more with correct stage returns True
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    # outbound_count 3 with wrong stage returns False
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=3, stage="PRE_VIEWING") is False


def test_should_share_phone_now_adaptive_type_allows_stage_or_drive_distance():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "adaptive"}
    # stage VIEWING_BOOKED returns True regardless of outbound_count or drive_distance_high
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    # drive_distance_high True and outbound_count >=1 returns True even if stage is None
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage=None, drive_distance_high=True) is True
    # drive_distance_high True but outbound_count 0 returns False
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage=None, drive_distance_high=True) is False
    # drive_distance_high False and stage None returns False
    assert should_share_phone_now(persona, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=5, stage=None, drive_distance_high=False) is False

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest

from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_or_responsive():
    # No landlord messages, previous is valid attitude
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="friendly") == "friendly"
    # No landlord messages, previous is invalid attitude
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="unknown") == "responsive"
    # No landlord messages, no previous
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([]) == "responsive"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_aggressive_overrides_others():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "Thanks for your interest!"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "Stop wasting my time please."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "I can show you tomorrow."},
    ]
    # Aggressive pattern should take precedence over friendly and helpful
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_time_gap_returns_slow_reply():
    now = datetime.now()
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "Hello", "created_at": now - timedelta(days=2)},
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "Yes", "created_at": now},
    ]
    # The gap between last two landlord messages is > 24 hours, so slow_reply
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "slow_reply"


def test_detect_landlord_attitude_cold_pattern_exact_match():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "body": "Ok"},
    ]
    # Exact match for cold pattern should return "cold"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "cold"

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude

def test_detect_landlord_attitude_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_or_default():
    # No landlord messages, previous is valid attitude
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="friendly") == "friendly"
    # No landlord messages, previous is invalid attitude, defaults to "responsive"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="unknown") == "responsive"
    # No landlord messages, no previous provided
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([]) == "responsive"

def test_detect_landlord_attitude_aggressive_pattern_overrides_others():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Thanks for your interest!"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Stop wasting my time please."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "I can show it tomorrow."},
    ]
    # Aggressive pattern should take precedence over friendly and helpful
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"

def test_detect_landlord_attitude_time_gap_returns_slow_reply():
    now = datetime.now()
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": now - timedelta(days=2)},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Are you interested?", "created_at": now},
    ]
    # More than 24 hours between last two landlord messages triggers slow_reply
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "slow_reply"

def test_detect_landlord_attitude_cold_pattern_exact_match():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Yes"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "No"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Ok"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Fine"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "When?"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "What time?"},
    ]
    # Only the last landlord message is checked for cold patterns
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([messages[-1]]) == "cold"
    # If last message is not cold, returns responsive
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([messages[0]]) == "cold"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Sure, thanks"}]) == "friendly"

from app.ai.personas import should_share_phone_now as phone_shared_state

def test_phone_shared_state_immediate_type_allows_early_sharing():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "immediate", "conversation_style": "direct_number_request"}
    # outbound_count 0 and 1 should allow sharing, 2 should not
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage=None) is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage=None) is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage=None) is False

def test_phone_shared_state_delayed_type_requires_viewing_booked_and_min_outbound():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"}
    # outbound_count less than 2 should not share even if stage is VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is False
    # outbound_count 2 and stage VIEWING_BOOKED should share
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=2, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    # stage not VIEWING_BOOKED should not share regardless of outbound_count
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=3, stage="PRE_VIEWING") is False

def test_phone_shared_state_viewing_first_type_shares_only_on_viewing_booked():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "viewing_first"}
    # Should share only if stage is VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=5, stage="PRE_VIEWING") is False

def test_phone_shared_state_adaptive_type_shares_on_viewing_or_drive_distance_and_outbound():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+447700900123", "phone_fetching_type": "adaptive"}
    # Should share if stage is VIEWING_BOOKED regardless of outbound_count
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="VIEWING_BOOKED") is True
    # Should share if drive_distance_high and outbound_count >= 1
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=1, stage="PRE_VIEWING", drive_distance_high=True) is True
    # Should not share if drive_distance_high but outbound_count is 0
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=0, stage="PRE_VIEWING", drive_distance_high=True) is False
    # Should not share if no viewing booked and no drive distance high
    assert phone_shared_state(persona, landlord_asked=False, phone_shared=False, outbound_count=5, stage="PRE_VIEWING", drive_distance_high=False) is False

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count

def test_outbound_count_empty_list():
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_outbound_count_none_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0

def test_outbound_count_mixed_senders():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Is it available?"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Yes"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "Please call me"},
        {"direction": "outbound", "message": "I will call you"},
        {"direction": "inbound", "message": "Thanks"},
        {"sender": "ai", "message": "Automated reply"},
        {"sender": "other", "message": "Ignore me"},
    ]
    # outbound_count should count user, tenant, outbound, ai only
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_outbound_count_case_insensitivity_and_missing_keys():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User", "message": "Hi"},  # uppercase mismatch, should not count
        {"sender": "USER", "message": "Hi"},  # uppercase match, should count
        {"direction": "Outbound", "message": "Hello"},  # uppercase mismatch, should not count
        {"direction": "outbound", "message": "Hello"},  # lowercase match, should count
        {"message": "No sender or direction"},  # no sender/direction, should not count
        {},  # empty dict, should not count
    ]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 2

from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages

def test_landlord_messages_filters_only_landlord_and_inbound():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Hi"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "Is it still available?"},
        {"sender": "user", "message": "Yes"},
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Please call me."},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Can I view it?"},
        {"sender": "agent", "message": "Agent message"},
        {"sender": None, "message": "No sender"},
        {"message": "No sender key"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "Is it still available?"},
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Please call me."},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Can I view it?"},
    ]
    result = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert result == expected

def test_landlord_messages_empty_and_none_input():
    assert landlord_messages([]) == []
    assert landlord_messages(None) == []

def test_landlord_messages_handles_missing_sender_key():
    messages = [
        {"message": "No sender key"},
        {"sender": "", "message": "Empty sender"},
        {"sender": None, "message": "None sender"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Valid landlord message"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Valid landlord message"},
    ]
    result = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert result == expected

def test_landlord_messages_case_insensitivity_of_sender():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Message 1"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Message 2"},
        {"sender": "Tenant", "message": "Message 3"},
        {"sender": "USER", "message": "Message 4"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Message 1"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Message 2"},
    ]
    result = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert result == expected
